creator: sobomax@FreeBSD.org
text:
> 
> On Wed, Sep 19, 2001 at 03:28:41AM -0700, sobomax@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> > This is a common problem of all packages that hardcode OS version number
> > in their installation paths. I'm not sure would it be worthwhile to fix
> > this or not. You can easily workarround the problem by recompiling/reinstalling
> > affected packages (librep and rep-gtk in this case) after upgrade.
> 
> The trouble is that *recompiling* rep-gtk won't help,
> it will still install into -4.3! You'd have to reinstall the entire upstream-
> dependencies of rep-gtk until you find the one who reports the old "4.3".

This is because you have to reinstall librep first.

> But I agree that it's kinda hard to fix, so we should settle with a closed
> but analyzed PR ;)

Ok, fine.

Thanks for reporting anyway!

-Maxim
